user-story.action: want a circuit breaker per provider+model that tracks failures and marks unhealthy providers
user-story.benefit: so that the fallback chain skips unhealthy providers and failure data feeds into diagnostics

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Shared error factory (createProviderError, isProviderError) extracted to packages/providers/src/errors.ts
openrouter-provider.ts and zen-mcp-provider.ts use the shared error factory instead of local copies
ProviderHealthTracker implements IProviderHealthTracker interface with circuit breaker and configurable thresholds
Sliding window failure tracking prunes old timestamps outside the window
Half-open state allows exactly ONE probe request; concurrent callers are blocked (thundering herd prevention)
Successful half-open probe fully closes circuit and resets failures
Failed half-open probe immediately re-opens circuit
recordFailure() accepts both plain Error and ProviderError (via type guard); non-retryable ProviderErrors are NOT counted toward circuit breaker
getStatus() returns JSON-serializable Record (not Map) with correct health/failure/circuit-open state for all tracked keys
Circuit breaker state is in-memory only. Process restart resets all state. This is an intentional design decision.
All existing provider tests continue to pass after error factory extraction (run full suite, not just new test files)
Constructor validates all numeric inputs (failureThreshold >= 1 integer, failureWindowMs >= 1000, circuitOpenDurationMs >= 1000, all finite)
Half-open probe timeout prevents permanently stuck half-open state (auto-resets to open after halfOpenProbeTimeoutMs)
maxTrackedKeys limit prevents unbounded memory growth (default: 1000)
failureTimestamps array is capped to failureThreshold * 2 to prevent O(N^2) degradation
recordFailure() short-circuits when circuit is already open (not half-open) -- no-op
reset(key) and clear() methods enable operational recovery and testing
Static buildKey(provider, model?) helper centralizes key format ("provider:model" or "provider:default")
Key validation rejects keys longer than 256 chars or with invalid characters
Optional onCircuitChange callback fires on state transitions for diagnostics integration
IProviderHealthTracker interface defined so ProviderChain (Story 9-5) depends on the interface, not the concrete class

component.name: errors.ts
component.description: Shared error factory extracted from duplicated code in openrouter-provider.ts and zen-mcp-provider.ts

function.name: isProviderError
function.description: Type guard: distinguishes ProviderError from plain Error by checking code (string), retryable (boolean), AND severity properties. The severity check reduces false positives from Node.js system errors that may coincidentally have code and retryable.

component.name: IProviderHealthTracker
component.description: Interface for the health tracker. Story 9-5's ProviderChain depends on this interface, not the concrete class.

component.name: ProviderHealthTracker
component.description: Circuit breaker per provider+model key. Implements IProviderHealthTracker. Tracks failures in a sliding window, opens circuit when threshold is breached, supports half-open probing with thundering herd prevention and probe timeout, enforces maxTrackedKeys limit, validates keys, and fires onCircuitChange callbacks.

method.name: constructor

parameter.name: options
parameter.type: { failureThreshold?: number; failureWindowMs?: number; circuitOpenDurationMs?: number; halfOpenProbeTimeoutMs?: number; maxTrackedKeys?: number; onCircuitChange?: (key: string, state: 'open' | 'half-open' | 'closed') => void }

method.validation: failureThreshold must be positive integer >= 1; failureWindowMs must be >= 1000; circuitOpenDurationMs must be >= 1000; halfOpenProbeTimeoutMs must be >= 1000; maxTrackedKeys must be positive integer >= 1; all must be finite numbers

method.name: static buildKey
method.description: Returns "provider:model" or "provider:default". Centralizes key format for use by ProviderChain (Story 9-5).

method.name: isHealthy
method.description: Returns true if key is healthy or if half-open probe is permitted. Key format: "provider:model". Validates key. Auto-resets stuck half-open probes after halfOpenProbeTimeoutMs.

method.name: recordFailure
method.description: Records a failure. Non-retryable ProviderErrors are NOT counted (config/caller problems, not health issues). If circuit already open (not half-open), short-circuits (no-op). Enforces maxTrackedKeys limit. Caps failureTimestamps to failureThreshold * 2. If in half-open state, immediately re-opens circuit. Otherwise adds to sliding window and checks threshold. Fires onCircuitChange on transitions.

method.name: recordSuccess
method.description: Records success. If half-open, fully closes circuit and resets failures. Fires onCircuitChange('closed') on transition.

method.name: getStatus
method.description: Returns JSON-serializable Record (not Map) with health status for all tracked provider+model keys within the current window.

method.name: reset
method.description: Deletes health state for a single key. Useful for operational recovery.

method.name: clear
method.description: Clears all health state. Useful for operational recovery and testing.

fields.field: Sliding window of failure epoch timestamps (capped to failureThreshold * 2)
Epoch ms when circuit should transition to half-open
Prevents thundering herd in half-open state
Epoch ms when half-open probe started. Used for probe timeout detection.

interface.name: IProviderHealthTracker
interface.description: Interface for the health tracker. ProviderChain (Story 9-5) depends on this, not the concrete class.
interface.methods: isHealthy, recordFailure, recordSuccess, getStatus, reset, clear

interface.name: HealthStatusEntry
interface.description: JSON-serializable status entry returned by getStatus()

interface.name: ProviderError
interface.description: Existing error interface extended from Error

defaults.default: Number of failures within window to trip circuit (must be >= 1, integer)
Sliding window duration in milliseconds (60s, must be >= 1000)
How long circuit stays open before half-open transition (300s / 5min, must be >= 1000)
How long before a stuck half-open probe auto-resets to open (30s, must be >= 1000)
Maximum number of provider+model keys tracked simultaneously (must be >= 1, integer)

reference.description: Contains local createProviderError() function (lines 21-32) and mapHttpError() that uses it. Must import from errors.ts after extraction.
Contains local createProviderError() function (lines 13-28) with slightly different return type annotation. Must import from errors.ts after extraction.
Defines ProviderError interface (line 296) and PROVIDER_ERROR_CODES constant (line 414). Both used by the error factory. Will also host IProviderHealthTracker and HealthStatusEntry interfaces.
Barrel exports. Must be updated to export createProviderError, isProviderError, ProviderHealthTracker, IProviderHealthTracker, and HealthStatusEntry.

phase.name: Task 1: Extract Shared Error Factory
phase.description: Create errors.ts with createProviderError() (with optional context/retryAfter params) and isProviderError() (with 'severity' in err check) extracted from duplicated provider code

components.component: Create packages/providers/src/errors.ts
Export createProviderError (with optional context and retryAfter parameters) and isProviderError (with severity check)
Write unit tests for both functions

phase.name: Task 2: Update Existing Providers
phase.description: Replace local createProviderError copies with imports from errors.ts

components.component: Update openrouter-provider.ts to import from ./errors.js
Update zen-mcp-provider.ts to import from ./errors.js
Delete local createProviderError functions
Verify ALL existing provider tests pass (full suite, not just modified files)

phase.name: Task 3: Implement ProviderHealthTracker
phase.description: Build the circuit breaker class implementing IProviderHealthTracker with sliding window, half-open probing with timeout, thundering herd prevention, constructor validation, key validation, maxTrackedKeys, failureTimestamps cap, non-retryable error skip, already-open short-circuit, onCircuitChange callback, reset/clear methods, and static buildKey helper

components.component: Add IProviderHealthTracker and HealthStatusEntry interfaces to packages/providers/src/types.ts
Create packages/providers/src/provider-health.ts implementing IProviderHealthTracker
Implement isHealthy(), recordFailure(), recordSuccess() with all enhancements
Implement static buildKey(), reset(), clear()
Sliding window failure tracking with timestamp pruning and capping
Half-open state with single-probe guard and probe timeout
Constructor validation, key validation, maxTrackedKeys limit
onCircuitChange callback on state transitions
Write comprehensive unit tests

phase.name: Task 4: Implement getStatus() Reporting and Finalize Exports
phase.description: Add getStatus() method returning Record (not Map), update barrel exports, write final tests, validate full suite

components.component: Implement getStatus() returning Record<string, HealthStatusEntry>
Update index.ts to export new modules and interfaces
Write getStatus() unit tests
Validate full test suite passes (all provider tests, not just new files)

integration.name: Provider Chain (Story 9-5)
integration.description: ProviderChain depends on IProviderHealthTracker interface (not concrete class). Calls isHealthy() before attempting a provider and recordFailure()/recordSuccess() after each attempt. Uses ProviderHealthTracker.buildKey() to construct keys.

integration.name: Provider Diagnostics (Story 9-2)
integration.description: Failure data from the health tracker feeds into diagnostics reporting. onCircuitChange callback enables diagnostics integration without coupling to DiagnosticsQueue.

integration.name: Agent Resolver (Story 9-8)
integration.description: RoleBasedAgentResolver uses health status to skip unhealthy providers in chain resolution

test-suite.name: errors.ts
test-suite.test: createProviderError returns ProviderError with correct code, message, retryable, severity
createProviderError defaults severity to 'medium' when not specified
createProviderError accepts optional context and retryAfter parameters
isProviderError returns true for ProviderError instances (checks code, retryable, AND severity)
isProviderError returns false for plain Error instances
isProviderError returns false for non-Error values (null, undefined, string, number)
isProviderError returns false for Error with code and retryable but missing severity (Node.js system error false positive prevention)

test-suite.name: ProviderHealthTracker
test-suite.test: 5 failures within 60s opens circuit
Failures outside window do not count (sliding window prunes old timestamps)
Circuit closes after 300s (half-open transition)
Half-open allows exactly ONE probe (halfOpenInProgress blocks concurrent probes)
Half-open probe success fully closes circuit and resets failures
Half-open probe failure immediately re-opens circuit
Half-open probe timeout auto-resets to open after halfOpenProbeTimeoutMs (prevents stuck half-open)
recordFailure() accepts both plain Error and ProviderError
recordFailure() with non-retryable ProviderError does NOT count toward circuit breaker
recordFailure() short-circuits when circuit already open (not half-open) -- no-op
recordFailure() caps failureTimestamps array to failureThreshold * 2
recordFailure() respects maxTrackedKeys limit (rejects new keys at capacity)
recordSuccess() resets all failure timestamps
getStatus() returns Record (not Map) with correct counts within window
reset(key) deletes single key; clear() deletes all keys
static buildKey('provider', 'model') returns 'provider:model'; buildKey('provider') returns 'provider:default'
Constructor throws on invalid failureThreshold (0, -1, 1.5, Infinity, NaN)
Constructor throws on invalid failureWindowMs (< 1000, Infinity, NaN)
Constructor throws on invalid circuitOpenDurationMs (< 1000, Infinity, NaN)
Key validation throws on keys > 256 chars or with invalid characters
onCircuitChange callback fires on open, half-open, and closed transitions
Unknown keys are reported as healthy by isHealthy()
Custom threshold/window/duration options are honored

regression-tests.test: All existing openrouter-provider tests pass after error factory extraction
All existing zen-mcp-provider tests pass after error factory extraction
Run ALL provider tests after errors.ts extraction (pnpm test --filter @tamma/providers), not just new test files

setting.name: failureThreshold
setting.description: Failures within window to trip circuit (must be >= 1, positive integer)

setting.name: failureWindowMs
setting.description: Sliding window size in ms (must be >= 1000)

setting.name: circuitOpenDurationMs
setting.description: Time circuit stays open before half-open (must be >= 1000)

setting.name: halfOpenProbeTimeoutMs
setting.description: Auto-reset stuck half-open probes after this duration (must be >= 1000)

setting.name: maxTrackedKeys
setting.description: Maximum tracked provider+model keys to prevent unbounded memory growth (must be >= 1, positive integer)

decision.title: In-memory only state (intentional)
decision.rationale: Circuit breaker state is in-memory only. Process restart resets all state. This is an intentional design decision. Provider outages are typically transient. Persisting circuit state adds complexity without significant benefit.

decision.title: Single-probe half-open guard with timeout
decision.rationale: Prevents thundering herd when circuit transitions from open to half-open. Only one caller gets through; all others are blocked until the probe completes. halfOpenProbeTimeoutMs (default 30s) auto-resets to open state if the probe caller crashes or forgets to call recordSuccess/recordFailure, preventing permanently stuck half-open state.

decision.title: Key format: "provider:model" with buildKey() helper
decision.rationale: Granular per-model tracking allows the same provider with different models to have independent health states (e.g., "openrouter:z-ai/z1-mini" vs "openrouter:anthropic/claude-opus-4"). Static buildKey() centralizes key construction for consistency across ProviderChain (Story 9-5) and other consumers.

decision.title: IProviderHealthTracker interface for dependency inversion
decision.rationale: ProviderChain (Story 9-5) depends on the IProviderHealthTracker interface, not the concrete ProviderHealthTracker class. This follows the interface-based design pattern from the architecture and enables testing with mock implementations.

decision.title: Non-retryable errors excluded from circuit breaker
decision.rationale: ProviderError instances with retryable === false indicate configuration or caller problems (e.g., invalid API key, unsupported model), not provider health issues. Counting them would incorrectly trip the circuit breaker for problems that won't be resolved by trying a different provider instance.

decision.title: getStatus() returns Record, not Map
decision.rationale: Record<string, HealthStatusEntry> is JSON-serializable, making it compatible with API responses, logging, and diagnostics without conversion. Map requires explicit serialization.

task.title: Extract Shared Error Factory
task.description: Create errors.ts with createProviderError() (with optional context/retryAfter params) and isProviderError() (with severity check), write unit tests

task.title: Update Existing Providers to Use Shared Error Factory
task.description: Replace local createProviderError in openrouter-provider.ts and zen-mcp-provider.ts with imports from errors.ts. Run ALL provider tests.

task.title: Implement ProviderHealthTracker with Circuit Breaker
task.description: Add IProviderHealthTracker interface to types.ts. Build ProviderHealthTracker class implementing the interface with all enhancements: constructor validation, key validation, maxTrackedKeys, failureTimestamps cap, non-retryable error skip, already-open short-circuit, half-open probe timeout, onCircuitChange callback, reset/clear, static buildKey.

task.title: Implement getStatus() Reporting (Record, not Map) and Finalize Exports
task.description: Add getStatus() method returning Record<string, HealthStatusEntry>, update barrel exports including IProviderHealthTracker and HealthStatusEntry, write final tests, validate full provider test suite

dependency.description: Story 9-3 has no upstream dependencies. It is in parallel Group 1 (no deps).
ProviderChain depends on IProviderHealthTracker interface (not concrete class) to skip unhealthy providers. Uses ProviderHealthTracker.buildKey() for key construction.

references.reference: Zen MCP provider with local createProviderError
Knowledge Base: Search spikes, bugs, findings, decisions
